g.text: A Probabilistic Framework for Machine Learning

g.data-narration: Bayesian learning treats model parameters as random variables with probability distributions, updating beliefs as data arrives.
g.text: A machine learning approach that treats parameters as
random variables with probability distributions, not fixed values.

g.data-narration: Unlike frequentist methods, Bayesian learning quantifies uncertainty and incorporates prior knowledge naturally.
Bayes' Theorem is the foundation: posterior equals likelihood times prior divided by evidence, updating our beliefs with data.
The posterior is updated belief after seeing data, likelihood shows how probable the data is under each hypothesis.
The counterintuitive medical example shows why Bayesian thinking matters: even a 99% accurate test yields only 50% probability with a rare disease.
Key differences: Bayesian treats parameters as random with distributions; frequentist treats them as fixed unknowns to estimate.
For classification, we compute posterior probabilities for each class given features and assign the class with highest probability.
Naïve Bayes assumes features are conditionally independent given the class, simplifying computation dramatically for high-dimensional data.
The algorithm has four steps: calculate priors, compute conditional probabilities, compute posteriors, and assign the class.
The spam filter example demonstrates how Naïve Bayes multiplies conditional probabilities to classify emails as spam or ham.
g.text: Example: Spam Filter with Naïve Bayes

g.data-narration: The Bayesian learning cycle: start with prior distribution, observe data to calculate likelihood, update to posterior, then repeat with new data.
g.text: Repeat: Posterior → Prior for next iteration

g.data-narration: Bayesian learning offers key advantages: incorporates prior knowledge, quantifies uncertainty, handles streaming data, and avoids overfitting.
Challenges include choosing appropriate priors, computational complexity for complex models, and careful model specification.
Real-world applications include spam filtering, medical diagnosis, document classification, recommendation systems, and genetic analysis.
Key takeaways: Bayesian learning uses probability distributions to update beliefs, treating parameters as random variables with prior and posterior.
g.text: • Parameters are random variables with probability distributions
• Naïve Bayes assumes conditional independence for tractability

g.data-narration: For exams: understand Bayes' theorem thoroughly, practice probability calculations, know the Naïve assumption, and watch for counterintuitive results.
g.text: and be able to apply it
